_meta.description: Sample HIGH confidence response — Boeing M10 bolt torque specification query

chunks: Table 4: Fastener Specifications (class 8.8) — M10 bolt maximum torque: 45 N·m (dry). For lubricated thread conditions apply torque reduction factor K=0.75.
Structural bolt installations must reference the current revision of the Fastener Specifications document. Torque values are specified per bolt grade and diameter.
Class 8.8 bolts are medium-carbon steel bolts suitable for high-stress structural applications. Torque specifications vary by bolt diameter: M8=23 N·m, M10=45 N·m, M12=78 N·m.
Thread lubrication affects effective clamping force. When lubricants are used, torque values from Table 4 must be adjusted using the K-factor specified in Section 6.2.
All field installation torque values must be verified against the most recent revision of Fastener_Specifications.pdf prior to use.
_test_input._logprob_note: 20 tokens: 16 at ln(0.861)≈-0.15 (common words), 4 at ln(0.638)≈-0.45 (technical terms). Mean token prob ≈ 0.816.

query: What is the maximum torque specification for M10 bolts in structural applications?
answer: The maximum torque specification for M10 class 8.8 bolts in structural applications is 45 N·m. This value applies to dry-thread conditions. For lubricated threads, apply a torque reduction factor of 0.75, resulting in a maximum of approximately 34 N·m. All fastener installations must comply with the values listed in Table 4 of Fastener_Specifications.pdf.
